NOTE: Do NOT manually edit this file. This file is automatically updated when semantic release (release.yml) runs. Manually editing this file can break your releases and break third party tools that use this file.

patches.name: Disable PairIP DRM
patches.description: Disables PairIP signature verification, Google Play LVL license check, System.exit kill switch, checksum integrity verification, and MHSDK analytics. Required for the app to launch after re-signing.

patches.name: Fix Firebase after re-signing
patches.description:
Fixes Firebase services (push notifications, Remote Config, Firebase Auth) that break after Morphe re-signs the app with a different certificate.

Apply with Original app certificate patch no other config needed.

patches.name: GmsCore support (MicroG)
patches.description:
Routes Google Play Services calls through MicroG instead of real GPS.

Works for: Google apps (YouTube, Maps, News, Photos) and third-party apps using classic Google Sign-In (Android 13 and below).

Does not work for: Android 14+ Credential Manager sign-in (most modern third-party apps), Play Integrity / SafetyNet checks, or apps with custom auth.

Requires MicroG RE installed. Apply with Original app certificate patch.

options.title: MicroG package name
options.description: Which MicroG / GmsCore app is installed on your device. Check your device's app list for "MicroG" or "GmsCore" if unsure — most users should leave this as the default.

options.title: Main activity class (optional)
options.description: The app's main Activity class name ending with a semicolon, e.g. /MainActivity; Used to inject the MicroG check at startup. Leave blank for auto-detection.

options.title: Custom package name (optional)
options.description: Rename the app to this package name. Leave blank to keep the original. Must be a valid package name (e.g. com.example.myapp).

patches.name: Provide Original app certificate
patches.description: By Default it Reads the signing certificate from the original app installed. Required for GmsCore, Firebase, and Signature spoof patches. Only fill options below if the original is uninstalled.

options.title: Path to original APK (if uninstalled)
options.description: Full path to the original APK or .apks bundle. Example: /sdcard/Download/Waze.apks

options.title: Certificate SHA-1 (manual)
options.description: 40-char hex. Found on APKMirror under app signature info.

options.title: Certificate Base64 DER (manual)
options.description: Needed only for Signature spoof patch. Get it with: apksigner verify --print-certs original.apk

patches.name: Spoof Widevine / DRM level
patches.description:
Reports Widevine L1 (hardware DRM) to apps that check DRM level locally.

Useful for apps that refuse to play HD/4K content on L3 devices or after re-signing.

Does not bypass server-side DRM - Netflix, Disney+ and similar are not affected.

options.title: Widevine security level to report
options.description: L1 = hardware DRM (highest quality). L3 = software only. Default L1.

options.title: HDCP level to report
options.description: HDCP_V2_2 is required for 4K content on most streaming apps.

patches.name: Spoof app signature
patches.description:
Makes the app think its signing certificate is unchanged after Morphe re-signs it.

Useful when an app crashes or shows a tamper warning because it checks its own certificate.

Does not bypass Play Integrity / SafetyNet hardware attestation.

Apply with Original app certificate patch.

options.title: Package name override (optional)
options.description: Leave blank — read automatically from the app. Only set this if you changed the app's package name and the patch is using the wrong one.

patches.name: Spoof install source
patches.description:
Makes the app think it was installed from a specific store (default: Google Play).

Useful when an app blocks features or shows errors because it detects it was not installed from the Play Store.

Only affects what the app itself sees - does not change the real system install record.

options.title: Store to impersonate
options.description: Most apps only check for the Google Play Store, so the default is usually correct. Pick from the list, or type any package name directly if your app checks for a different store.

patches.name: Unlock Premium
patches.description: Unlocks all premium features by forcing entitlement status to SUBSCRIPTION and making all premium gating checks return true.
